description: Add configSchema with optional instanceUrl to the GitLab plugin so users with self-hosted GitLab CE/EE instances can configure their custom domain. The existing gitlab.com SaaS pattern is preserved.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/gitlab && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add configSchema with instanceUrl to GitLab plugin
userStories.description: Add a configSchema property with an optional instanceUrl field of type 'url' to the GitLab plugin class and package.json opentabs field. Keep existing SaaS urlPatterns so the plugin works out of the box for gitlab.com users.
acceptanceCriteria: GitLabPlugin class has `override readonly configSchema: ConfigSchema` with an `instanceUrl` field of type 'url', required: false
package.json opentabs field has a `configSchema` object with the same `instanceUrl` definition
Existing `urlPatterns: ['*://gitlab.com/*']` is preserved in both src/index.ts and package.json
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/gitlab/src/index.ts (add configSchema property and ConfigSchema import)
- plugins/gitlab/package.json (add configSchema to opentabs field)

Context:
The Grafana plugin is the reference implementation for self-hosted URL config. See plugins/grafana/src/index.ts. However, Grafana has `urlPatterns: []` and `required: true` because it's purely self-hosted. GitLab is different — it has a well-known SaaS domain (gitlab.com) AND self-hosted instances. The pattern here: keep SaaS urlPatterns, add OPTIONAL instanceUrl config.

Changes:

1. In plugins/gitlab/src/index.ts:
   - Change `import type { ToolDefinition } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';` to `import type { ConfigSchema, ToolDefinition } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';`
   - Add after `override readonly homepage = 'https://gitlab.com';`:
     ```
     override readonly configSchema: ConfigSchema = {
       instanceUrl: {
         type: 'url' as const,
         label: 'GitLab URL',
         description: 'The URL of your self-hosted GitLab instance (e.g., https://gitlab.example.com). Leave empty to use gitlab.com.',
         required: false,
         placeholder: 'https://gitlab.example.com',
       },
     };
     ```
   - Do NOT change urlPatterns — keep `readonly urlPatterns = ['*://gitlab.com/*'];`
   - Do NOT change homepage — keep `override readonly homepage = 'https://gitlab.com';`

2. In plugins/gitlab/package.json:
   - Add `configSchema` to the `opentabs` object:
     ```json
     "configSchema": {
       "instanceUrl": {
         "type": "url",
         "label": "GitLab URL",
         "description": "The URL of your self-hosted GitLab instance (e.g., https://gitlab.example.com). Leave empty to use gitlab.com.",
         "required": false,
         "placeholder": "https://gitlab.example.com"
       }
     }
     ```
   - Keep existing urlPatterns and homepage unchanged

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove or change the existing `urlPatterns` — SaaS users depend on it
- Do NOT set `required: true` — the SaaS domain must work without configuration
- Do NOT modify any tool files, API files, or isReady logic
- The configSchema must be identical in src/index.ts and package.json (except for `as const` type assertion in TS)
